roadmap_checkpoints.label: HTML5 Foundations
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master the building blocks of the web. You'll learn how browsers parse HTML, semantic element choices that affect SEO and accessibility, and how to structure a document that's both human-readable and machine-friendly.
learning_goals: Understand the role of semantic HTML elements (header, main, article, section)
Build well-structured forms with proper labels and input types
Apply accessibility best practices using ARIA roles and alt text
Validate HTML using the W3C validator and fix common errors

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Advanced CSS & Grid
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Go beyond basic styling. This checkpoint covers CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts, responsive design with media queries, CSS custom properties (variables), and modern techniques like clamp() and container queries.
learning_goals: Build complex two-dimensional layouts using CSS Grid
Create flexible, one-dimensional layouts with Flexbox
Write responsive styles using media queries and fluid units
Use CSS custom properties to build a maintainable design token system

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Layouts Master Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Test your knowledge of HTML structure and CSS layouts. This quiz covers semantic markup, Flexbox, Grid, and responsive design patterns encountered in real-world projects.
learning_goals: Recall the difference between block, inline, and inline-block elements
Identify correct Grid and Flexbox properties for given layout challenges
Diagnose and fix common layout bugs
Apply media query breakpoints to a given design spec

roadmap_checkpoints.label: JS Logic & Variables
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn the fundamentals of JavaScript: data types, variable scoping (var vs let vs const), operators, control flow, and functions. This is the foundation for all JS-powered interactivity.
learning_goals: Distinguish between let, const, and var and when to use each
Write functions using both declaration and arrow-function syntax
Control program flow using if/else, switch, and ternary expressions
Understand truthy/falsy values and short-circuit evaluation

roadmap_checkpoints.label: DOM & Events
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Bring web pages to life by manipulating the DOM and responding to user events. Covers querySelector, event listeners, event propagation, and the difference between innerHTML and textContent.
learning_goals: Select and modify DOM elements using querySelector and querySelectorAll
Attach and remove event listeners for user interactions
Explain event bubbling and capture, and use stopPropagation when needed
Dynamically create and remove elements from the page

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Interactive Site Project
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Apply everything learned so far to build a small interactive website — a to-do list, a quiz app, or a weather dashboard. Focus on clean HTML structure, responsive CSS, and vanilla JS logic with no frameworks.
learning_goals: Plan a small project by breaking it into HTML structure, CSS styles, and JS logic
Implement CRUD operations (add, edit, delete items) using DOM manipulation
Persist data across page reloads using localStorage
Deploy the project to GitHub Pages or Netlify

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Asynchronous JS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand how JavaScript handles async operations: the event loop, callbacks, Promises, and async/await syntax. Learn to fetch data from APIs and handle loading and error states gracefully.
learning_goals: Explain the JavaScript event loop and call stack
Chain Promises using .then()/.catch() and convert to async/await
Fetch data from a public REST API and render it in the DOM
Handle network errors and display appropriate user feedback

roadmap_checkpoints.label: React Fundamentals
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Introduction to React: JSX syntax, functional components, props, and the useState and useEffect hooks. Build a small component-driven UI and understand the virtual DOM and reconciliation.
learning_goals: Write functional components using JSX and pass data via props
Manage local component state with the useState hook
Trigger side effects and data fetching with useEffect
Compose a multi-component page with a clear data-flow hierarchy

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Learn a Programming Language
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This module introduces a foundational programming language (e.g., Python, Go, or Bash) essential for scripting, automation, and interacting with APIs in cloud environments. You will learn core syntax, data structures, and program flow.
learning_goals: Understand basic programming concepts like variables, loops, and conditionals.
Write simple scripts to automate common administrative tasks.
Demonstrate knowledge of functions and modular programming.
Be able to debug and troubleshoot basic code snippets.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Ubuntu / Debian
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to navigate, configure, and manage servers running Ubuntu or Debian, two of the most prevalent Linux distributions in cloud infrastructure. This module covers fundamental command-line operations, package management, and user administration.
learning_goals: Perform basic file system navigation and manipulation.
Manage software packages using `apt` and `dpkg`.
Understand user and group management, including permissions.
Configure basic network settings and services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Learn a Programming Language + Ubuntu / Debian Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz assesses your understanding of fundamental programming concepts and your ability to manage Ubuntu/Debian systems. It combines theoretical knowledge with practical scenarios, ensuring you can apply learned skills.
learning_goals: Validate basic programming logic and syntax from the chosen language.
Demonstrate proficiency with core Linux commands and system navigation.
Solve common administrative problems on Ubuntu/Debian.
Reinforce key concepts from both the programming and Linux modules.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FreeBSD
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore FreeBSD, a robust Unix-like operating system known for its stability, advanced networking, and ZFS file system capabilities. This module covers its distinct architecture, package management (`pkg`), and system configuration.
learning_goals: Differentiate FreeBSD from Linux in terms of architecture and philosophy.
Manage software packages using the `pkg` system.
Perform basic system configuration and service management.
Understand the fundamentals of the ZFS file system.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: NetBSD
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Delve into NetBSD, a highly portable and secure Unix-like operating system that runs on a wide range of hardware. Learn its unique `pkgsrc` package management system and kernel configuration, emphasizing its focus on clean design and portability.
learning_goals: Understand NetBSD's emphasis on portability and security.
Manage software packages using the `pkgsrc` system.
Perform basic system and network configuration.
Identify key differences and use cases compared to other Unix-like systems.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Forward Proxy
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the concept of a forward proxy, its role in network security, content filtering, and anonymity. Learn how to configure and deploy a basic forward proxy server to manage outgoing network traffic.
learning_goals: Define what a forward proxy is and its common use cases.
Explain the benefits and potential drawbacks of using a forward proxy.
Configure a basic forward proxy server (e.g., using Squid).
Troubleshoot common issues related to proxy server configuration and connectivity.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Tomcat
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to install, configure, and manage Apache Tomcat, a widely used open-source web server and servlet container for Java applications. This module covers deploying web applications, configuring server parameters, and basic performance tuning.
learning_goals: Install and set up Apache Tomcat on a server.
Deploy web applications (WAR files) to Tomcat.
Understand the key configuration files (e.g., `server.xml`, `web.xml`).
Monitor basic Tomcat performance and troubleshoot common issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FreeBSD + NetBSD + Forward Proxy + Tomcat Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This comprehensive quiz evaluates your grasp of alternative Unix-like operating systems (FreeBSD, NetBSD) and foundational networking/application server concepts (Forward Proxy, Tomcat). It tests your ability to compare, contrast, and apply these technologies.
learning_goals: Differentiate core features and use cases for FreeBSD and NetBSD.
Identify appropriate scenarios for deploying a forward proxy.
Demonstrate understanding of basic Tomcat deployments and configurations.
Integrate knowledge of operating systems, networking, and application servers.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IIS
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to install, configure, and manage Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS), a powerful web server for Windows environments. This covers hosting websites, managing application pools, and understanding key security features.
learning_goals: Install and configure IIS on a Windows server.
Host and manage multiple websites using IIS.
Understand and manage IIS application pools.
Implement basic security settings and access controls within IIS.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Caching Server
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the principles of caching and learn how to implement and manage caching servers to improve application performance, reduce database load, and enhance user experience. Explore different caching strategies and technologies.
learning_goals: Explain the benefits and types of caching in web applications.
Deploy and configure a caching server (e.g., Varnish, Redis).
Implement effective caching policies and invalidation strategies.
Monitor cache performance and identify optimization opportunities.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Load Balancer
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore the concept of load balancing and its critical role in distributing traffic across multiple servers for high availability, scalability, and improved performance. Learn about different load balancing algorithms and deployment strategies.
learning_goals: Define load balancing and explain its importance in cloud infrastructure.
Describe common load balancing algorithms (e.g., Round Robin, Least Connections).
Configure a basic software-based load balancer (e.g., Nginx, HAProxy).
Understand health checks and session persistence in load balancing.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer Project #1
roadmap_checkpoints.description: In this hands-on project, you will design and implement a multi-tier web application architecture. You'll integrate a web server (IIS or Tomcat), a caching layer, and a load balancer to serve a simple application, applying system administration and networking skills.
learning_goals: Design a scalable and highly available application architecture.
Deploy and configure web server, caching server, and load balancer components.
Integrate components to create a functional, optimized system.
Troubleshoot complex issues across multiple infrastructure layers.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Cloud
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Get started with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), one of the leading public cloud providers. Learn its core services like Compute Engine for virtual machines, Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) for networking, Cloud Storage, and Identity and Access Management (IAM).
learning_goals: Navigate the Google Cloud Console and understand its interface.
Provision and manage virtual machines using Compute Engine.
Configure basic networking within a VPC, including firewalls.
Understand and manage IAM roles and permissions for cloud resources.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Digital Ocean
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore DigitalOcean, a developer-friendly cloud provider known for its simplicity and affordability. Focus on Droplets (VMs), Spaces (object storage), Managed Databases, and basic networking to quickly deploy and manage scalable applications.
learning_goals: Create and manage virtual machines (Droplets) on DigitalOcean.
Utilize DigitalOcean Spaces for object storage.
Configure basic networking for Droplets.
Understand the DigitalOcean interface and resource management.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Google Cloud + Digital Ocean Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz tests your foundational knowledge of two distinct cloud providers: Google Cloud and DigitalOcean. It assesses your ability to identify core services, deployment methods, and management paradigms on each platform.
learning_goals: Compare and contrast key compute, storage, and networking services across GCP and DigitalOcean.
Identify the best use cases or scenarios for each cloud platform.
Demonstrate understanding of resource provisioning and management in both environments.
Reinforce cloud-agnostic concepts applied to specific providers.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Hetzner
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn about Hetzner, a German hosting provider renowned for its robust dedicated servers and competitively priced cloud instances. Focus on provisioning cloud servers, configuring networks, and understanding their infrastructure-as-a-service model.
learning_goals: Provision and manage virtual servers on Hetzner Cloud.
Understand the differences between dedicated and cloud servers at Hetzner.
Configure network settings for Hetzner instances.
Manage resources through the Hetzner Cloud Console.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Heroku
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Discover Heroku, a platform-as-a-service (PaaS) that significantly simplifies application deployment and scaling. Learn about buildpacks, dynos, and add-ons, and how to leverage Heroku for a streamlined developer experience without managing underlying infrastructure.
learning_goals: Deploy an application to Heroku using Git.
Understand the concepts of dynos, buildpacks, and slugs.
Utilize Heroku Add-ons for services like databases.
Manage environment variables and application configurations on Heroku.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Contabo
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Contabo, another European provider offering competitive pricing for VPS and dedicated servers. Learn about their infrastructure offerings, how to provision resources, and perform initial server setup for various operating systems.
learning_goals: Provision and manage a Virtual Private Server (VPS) on Contabo.
Understand Contabo's pricing structure and resource allocation.
Perform initial operating system setup and configuration.
Manage server resources and access through the Contabo portal.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: FTP / SFTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the File Transfer Protocol (FTP) and, more importantly, the Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) for transferring files between systems. Learn to configure servers and clients for secure, efficient, and authenticated file transfers.
learning_goals: Differentiate between FTP and SFTP, emphasizing security implications.
Configure an SFTP server for secure file access.
Use an SFTP client to transfer files securely.
Understand user authentication and permissions for file transfers.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Hetzner + Heroku + Contabo + FTP / SFTP Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz covers a range of hosting solutions from Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) providers like Hetzner and Contabo, to Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) like Heroku, along with essential file transfer protocols (FTP/SFTP). It assesses your understanding of diverse deployment environments and secure data movement.
learning_goals: Compare and contrast IaaS and PaaS models in practical scenarios.
Identify appropriate hosting solutions based on project requirements.
Demonstrate knowledge of secure and efficient file transfer methods.
Integrate understanding of different cloud and hosting platforms.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: HTTP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Dive deep into the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP), the stateless foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web. Understand HTTP methods (GET, POST), status codes, headers, and the request/response cycle.
learning_goals: Explain the fundamental HTTP request and response cycle.
Identify common HTTP methods and their semantic meaning.
Interpret various HTTP status codes and their implications.
Understand the purpose and structure of common HTTP headers.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: White / Grey Listing
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn about whitelisting and greylisting as effective security mechanisms, particularly for email and network access control. Understand their implementation, configuration, and effectiveness in preventing unwanted or malicious traffic.
learning_goals: Define whitelisting and greylisting and explain their operational principles.
Identify use cases where whitelisting or greylisting would be beneficial.
Understand how to configure basic whitelisting for network access.
Explain the security benefits and potential drawbacks of these techniques.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: IMAP
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Understand the Internet Message Access Protocol (IMAP), used by email clients to retrieve and manage messages directly on a mail server. Learn about its features like folder management, synchronization across devices, and persistent storage on the server.
learning_goals: Explain IMAP's role in email communication and client-server interaction.
Compare and contrast IMAP with other email protocols like POP3.
Understand how IMAP supports folder management and message synchronization.
Configure an email client to connect to an IMAP server.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer Project #2
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This project challenges you to set up a resilient web application deployed across at least two different cloud providers or hosting solutions. You'll implement network security rules, configure secure data transfer, and ensure high availability, showcasing multi-cloud infrastructure management skills.
learning_goals: Deploy and manage applications across heterogeneous cloud/hosting environments.
Implement robust network security policies (firewalls, access lists).
Configure and utilize secure file transfer methods for deployment and updates.
Demonstrate ability to manage and troubleshoot a distributed application.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Domain Keys
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn about DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) and its crucial role in email authentication to combat spoofing, phishing, and spam. Understand how DKIM works by cryptographically signing outgoing emails and how to configure and verify DKIM records for your domains.
learning_goals: Explain the purpose of DKIM in email authentication.
Understand how DKIM signatures are generated and verified.
Configure DKIM records (TXT records) for a domain.
Verify the integrity and authenticity of DKIM-signed emails.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: OSI Model
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Master the Open Systems Interconnection (OSI) model, a conceptual framework that standardizes the functions of a telecommunication or computing system. Understand its seven layers, their distinct responsibilities, and how they interact to facilitate network communication.
learning_goals: List and describe all seven layers of the OSI model.
Explain the function and protocols associated with each layer.
Understand how data flows through the OSI model from application to physical layer.
Apply the OSI model to troubleshoot network connectivity and application issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Netlify
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Netlify, a leading platform for modern web development that offers continuous deployment, serverless functions, and a global CDN. Learn to deploy static sites, Single Page Applications (SPAs), and dynamic applications with ease, leveraging Git-based workflows.
learning_goals: Deploy a website or application using Netlify's continuous deployment.
Understand Netlify's build process and automated deployments.
Utilize Netlify Functions for serverless backend logic.
Manage custom domains, SSL certificates, and environment variables on Netlify.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Domain Keys + OSI Model + Netlify Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz combines email security best practices (Domain Keys), fundamental networking theory (OSI Model), and modern web deployment strategies (Netlify). It assesses your ability to connect diverse technical concepts and apply them in real-world infrastructure scenarios.
learning_goals: Validate understanding of email authentication mechanisms like DKIM.
Apply OSI model concepts to analyze and troubleshoot network issues.
Demonstrate proficiency with modern web hosting and deployment platforms.
Integrate knowledge of network layers, security, and application delivery.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Secret Management
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn best practices for securely storing, accessing, and managing sensitive information like API keys, database credentials, and certificates in cloud and on-premises environments. Explore tools and strategies to protect secrets throughout their lifecycle.
learning_goals: Understand the principles of secure secret management.
Identify common risks associated with insecure secret handling.
Explore popular secret management tools (e.g., Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
Implement secure practices for secret injection and rotation in applications.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Grafana
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Get proficient with Grafana, an open-source platform for data visualization and monitoring. Learn to create interactive dashboards, connect to various data sources (e.g., Prometheus, InfluxDB, CloudWatch), and configure alerts for proactive incident response.
learning_goals: Install and configure Grafana and connect data sources.
Build insightful dashboards with various visualization panels.
Write PromQL or other query languages to extract meaningful metrics.
Set up and manage alerting rules for critical infrastructure events.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Secret Management + Grafana Quiz
roadmap_checkpoints.description: This quiz focuses on two critical areas for cloud infrastructure engineers: securing sensitive data through Secret Management and ensuring system health through robust Monitoring with Grafana. It tests your knowledge of both theoretical concepts and practical tool usage.
learning_goals: Validate understanding of best practices for secret lifecycle management.
Demonstrate proficiency in building and interpreting Grafana dashboards.
Identify secure solutions for storing and retrieving application secrets.
Apply monitoring concepts to identify and diagnose system issues.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Docker Swarm
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Learn to deploy and manage containerized applications using Docker Swarm, Docker's native orchestration tool. Understand how to initialize a swarm, deploy services, scale applications, and perform rolling updates for high availability.
learning_goals: Initialize and manage a Docker Swarm cluster.
Deploy and manage services within a Docker Swarm.
Scale containerized applications up and down.
Perform rolling updates and rollbacks for services.

roadmap_checkpoints.label: Nexus
roadmap_checkpoints.description: Explore Nexus Repository Manager, a universal artifact repository that supports various formats like Maven, npm, Docker, NuGet, and more. Learn to set up and manage private repositories to store, proxy, and distribute software components efficiently.
learning_goals: Install and configure Nexus Repository Manager.
Create and manage different types of repositories (proxy, hosted, group).
Push and pull artifacts to and from Nexus repositories.
Integrate Nexus into CI/CD pipelines for artifact management.

tasks.title: HTML Elements
tasks.description: Learn the core HTML elements and how to structure a semantic web page.

tasks.title: CSS Styling
tasks.description: Apply styles using selectors, the box model, and Flexbox/Grid layouts.

tasks.title: Javascript Components
tasks.description: Build interactive UI components using vanilla JavaScript and DOM events.

tasks.title: Python Fundamentals
tasks.description: Explore Python syntax, data structures, and scripting basics.

achievements.title: Zari's Movie Binge
achievements.description: Complete 5 lessons in a single day

achievements.title: Eddy's Wood Carving Craft
achievements.description: Finish a project checkpoint from start to finish

cp-003.question: Which property allows a nested grid to align its tracks exactly with those of its parent container?
cp-003.explanation: The grid-template-columns: subgrid; property allows a nested grid to align its tracks with its parent container.

cp-003.question: Which CSS feature is best for changing a component's layout based on the width of its own parent container?
cp-003.explanation: Container Queries are used to change a component's layout based on the width of its own parent container.

cp-003.question: If an element has width: 300px, padding: 20px, and box-sizing: border-box, what is its total rendered width?
cp-003.explanation: With box-sizing: border-box, the padding is included in the width, so the total rendered width remains 300px.

cp-003.question: What is the most semantic HTML5 element for a group of introductory content or navigation links?
cp-003.explanation: The <header> element is specifically designed for introductory content, logos, and navigational aids.

cp-003.question: How can you manage style priority without relying on high-specificity selectors or !important?
cp-003.explanation: Cascade Layers (@layers) allow you to manage style priority without relying on high-specificity selectors or !important.
